description: Xiamen's most beautiful coastline, a cycling paradise and a photography hotspot with tropical vibes.
A natural beach loved by Xiamen locals, featuring fine sand, clear waters, and a relatively tranquil atmosphere.
A famous seaside attraction along Huandao Road, where coconut groves and modern sculptures complement each other, making it an ideal starting point for cycling and ocean viewing.
A burgeoning cultural and creative hub in Xiamen, home to art studios, cafes, and trendy shops.
Xiamen's aerial sightseeing cableway, connecting Wanshi Botanical Garden and Zhonggu Mountain, offering panoramic city views.
A former fishing village transformed into an artistic neighborhood, lined with small shops and filled with a literary atmosphere.
The highest peak on Gulangyu Island, the best vantage point in Xiamen, offering a 360-degree panoramic view of the city.
An academic institution founded by Mr. Tan Kah Kee, showcasing unique Tan Kah Kee architectural style and rich cultural heritage.
A modern seaside resort area with well-equipped beach facilities and expansive, stunning ocean views.
The only piano-themed museum in China, housing antique pianos from around the world, symbolizing the Island of Music.
A large park showcasing the essence of domestic and international garden art, with rich wetland ecology.
A maritime garden blending Eastern and Western styles, featuring a piano museum and beautiful scenery.
The mausoleum of Mr. Tan Kah Kee, a cultural landscape park integrating commemoration, garden design, and ocean views.
The main commercial street on Gulangyu Island, offering a variety of Minnan specialty snacks and souvenir shops.
Xiamen's most popular urban beach, perfect for enjoying ocean views, sunsets, and relaxation.
The site of the Zheng Chenggong Statue, a seaside park with scenic views and a memorial to the national hero.
